description.p: qtIDM is a Linux-native download manager built with Qt. It provides segmented transfers, persistent queues, scheduling, browser integration, media handling, checksum verification, and guarded archive extraction.
Downloads can be organized into queues and categories, resumed across sessions, and controlled with configurable connection, retry, proxy, and conflict-handling policies.

Adds stable transfer-rate and ETA reporting, session statistics, live alternate speed limits, embedded static icons, refreshed application identity and branding, and a matching dark browser extension interface.

Adds complete request persistence, configurable transfer policies, browser-extension packaging, guarded extraction, Secret Service credentials, metered-network handling, and maintained social-site extraction.
